reforms.description: The Swiss government launched a 'growth package' in 2004 aimed at boosting productivity through a series of measures, including a proposed 6% increase in federal funds for education, research, and innovation, as well as a 7% growth in funding for the Commission for Technology and Innovation (CTI) to stimulate applied research. Many of these measures are still pending legislative approval or have just taken effect.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research and innovation is expected to enhance productivity and stimulate applied research activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures are still pending legislative approval or have just taken effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in public funding for research and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Council submitted a proposal to Parliament suggesting a 6% increase in federal funds for education, research, and innovation.

reforms.description: The Valida project was launched to validate the acquired experience and skills of workers, particularly focusing on those with foreign qualifications, allowing them to access Swiss qualifications more easily.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to Swiss qualifications for skilled workers, the project aims to enhance the human capital available for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The project has been launched and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The project represents a significant initiative to improve the recognition of skills and qualifications.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal government has also launched the Valida project – a system for validating acquired experience.
